my_thoughts: The correction/refinement works: misquote (not 'all emotions at same time'), refines: conceptual scope (conscience = feeling emotions together, not simultaneously), provides: functional definition (conscience = feeling emotions together). The student cites misquote (stuck on literal impossibility), which shows they understand: all emotions at same time = impossible (literal impossibility). They'll likely accept the correction/refinement. Single-turn episode: literal impossibility question → correction/refinement → complete. Hypothesis: literal impossibility corrections lead to compliance in single-turn episodes—testable by tracking whether literal impossibility corrections end episodes.

visible_response:
No, I never said that.
I said that in a moment of conscience, whether you wish it or
not, you will feel all your emotions on the same subject at once.
But it is not a
definition of conscience. Conscience can be defined as an emotional feeling of truth
on a given subject.
